design_philosophy.core_loop: Place disks on an 8×8 board to flip opponent pieces; maximize your color count by game's end
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Strategic position control vs. immediate piece advantage; long-term board dominance vs. short-term gains
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Outwit an opponent through superior tactical foresight and board reading; master a timeless abstract strategy game
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Zero-luck, perfect information abstract strategy with elegant simplicity masking deep tactical complexity. Every move is reversible in consequence but irreversible in game state—forcing deliberate, consequence-aware play. The game emphasizes observation and strategy over pattern memorization, rewarding positional mastery over execution skill.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Difficulty scales entirely through opponent AI strength (NES version likely offers multiple difficulty levels). Player can control game pace and study board state before committing moves, enabling skill expression through tactical depth rather than reflexive execution.

progression.unlock_mechanism: Progression likely tied to defeating AI opponents of increasing difficulty levels
replayability_hooks: Infinite game variety from 8×8 board permutations
Every game produces unique board states from identical rules

world_building_techniques: Shakespearean naming reference (Othello, the Moor of Venice) reflects conflict and reversal themes
Visual metaphor: green board evokes military campaign field; black/white pieces echo Othello's dramatic color symbolism

faction_indicators: Light vs. Dark piece ownership creates binary team identification
Piece capture mechanic (flipping opponent disks) symbolizes dominance and conversion
Turn-based structure enforces shared temporal parity between players
danger_signals: Positional encirclement (being surrounded limits valid moves)
Minority piece count on board (visible power imbalance)
Forced pass conditions (inability to move signals strategic disadvantage)
environmental_storytelling: Board density (empty squares vs. occupied) communicates game progression stage
Piece distribution patterns reveal strategic control zones
Green field backdrop reinforces competitive battlefield framing

sources_used: Wikipedia: Reversi article (history, rules, Othello variant description, naming/thematic context)
